title: The identity function is bijective
informal_statement: For every type alpha, the identity function id : alpha -> alpha is bijective (both injective and surjective).
informal_proof_idea: Injectivity: id a = id b unfolds definitionally to a = b, so the hypothesis is already the goal. Surjectivity: for any target b, b itself is a preimage since id b = b by rfl.
formal_statement_pp: theorem id_bijective : ∀ (α : Type), Function.Bijective (id : α → α)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 7bce298c). Step 1 kernel-failed: a bulleted (`constructor; · ...; · ...`) tactic proof lost its case-block structure under the default `flat_tactic_sequence` transport (the tactic-transport hazard documented in docs/proofsearch-integration.md), leaving `hab` out of scope for the following line. Step 2 fixed it by switching to a single-line term-mode proof (`exact <fun a b hab => hab, fun b => <b, rfl>>`), avoiding bullets entirely, and reached kernel_verified. Not packaged as a separate negative example: same category of authoring/transport slip as the injective_comp binder-arity issue, not an independent tactic-mismatch lesson. Picked after a concurrent agent had already claimed both linear_injective and strictMono_injective from packets/elementary/functions/QUEUE.md; closes the id_bijective item there.
